description: Manages TCP connections for FIX protocol engines including server-side acceptors, client-side initiators, SSL/TLS encryption, automatic reconnection, and socket configuration

actors.name: Socket Acceptor
actors.description: Server-side component that binds to a port and accepts incoming TCP connections from trading counterparties

actors.name: Socket Initiator
actors.description: Client-side component that establishes outbound TCP connections to remote FIX servers

actors.name: SSL/TLS Layer
actors.description: Optional TLS termination layer wrapping plain socket connections for encrypted FIX messaging

actors.name: Application Layer
actors.description: Business application that receives connection lifecycle events

values.name: stopped
values.description: Connection layer is not running; no sockets open

values.name: pending
values.description: Initiator is attempting to establish TCP connection to target

values.name: connected
values.description: TCP connection established; sessions can begin logon

values.name: disconnected
values.description: Connection was established but has dropped; reconnect will be attempted

transitions.description: start() called on initiator; begins outbound connection attempt
TCP handshake completed; connection ready for FIX logon
Connection attempt timed out or refused
Network error or remote peer closed connection
Reconnect interval elapsed; initiator attempts reconnection

acceptor_binding: Acceptor binds to SocketAcceptPort and listens for incoming connections
Each accepted connection is mapped to a session based on SenderCompID/TargetCompID in the Logon message
SocketReuseAddress=Y allows the acceptor to restart without waiting for OS port release
initiator_reconnection: On connection loss, initiator waits ReconnectInterval seconds before reattempting
Multiple target hosts can be configured (SocketConnectHost1, SocketConnectHost2, etc.) for failover
HostSelectionPolicy controls which host is tried first and how priority-based failover works
ssl_security: SSL sessions use the same configuration as plain sessions but wrap the socket in TLS
Certificate and private key paths must be configured before starting
CA certificate path enables mutual TLS (mTLS) peer verification
SSL and non-SSL sessions cannot mix on the same port
socket_tuning: TCP_NODELAY disables Nagle's algorithm for sub-millisecond message delivery; recommended for low-latency trading
Buffer sizes should be tuned based on expected message throughput; larger buffers reduce system calls
threading: Each connection runs I/O on a dedicated thread pool; session callbacks are invoked from I/O threads
Applications sharing state across sessions must synchronize access or use SynchronizedApplication wrapper

given: configured SocketAcceptPort is already in use
port_bind_failed.result: Acceptor startup fails with ConfigError; application is notified

given: certificate verification or cipher negotiation fails
ssl_handshake_failed.result: Connection dropped; initiator will retry after reconnect interval

given: target host refuses the connection or is unreachable
connection_refused.result: Connection attempt fails; initiator waits ReconnectInterval before retrying

given: session is in connected or logged_on state
TCP socket receives error or zero-byte read (peer closed connection)
connection_lost.result: Session is disconnected; initiator begins reconnect cycle

given: initiator completes TCP handshake OR acceptor accepts incoming connection
SSL handshake succeeds (if SSL is enabled)
connection_established.result: TCP connection ready; FIX session logon can begin

given: application calls stop() on acceptor or initiator
graceful_stop.result: All sessions are logged out and sockets closed cleanly

errors.message: Failed to bind to configured port

events.name: fix.connection.established
events.description: TCP connection (and optional TLS) successfully established

related.reason: FIX sessions are established on top of connections managed here
Message store maintains sequences across connection drops and recoveries

goals.description: Manages TCP connections for FIX protocol engines including server-side acceptors, client-side initiators, SSL/TLS encryption, automatic reconnection, and socket configuration

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when dependencies are unavailable

tradeoffs.reason: integration failures can cascade across systems
